2014-01-01.title: Egypt Says 3 Journalists Will Be Held 15 More Days
2014-01-01.abstract: The journalists from Al Jazeera English face charges that include belonging to a terrorist group and harming Egypt’s reputation abroad.

2014-01-01.title: Cameroon: Kidnapped French Priest Is Released
2014-01-01.abstract: Georges Vandenbeusch, 42, who was reported to have been held in Nigeria by Boko Haram, a group with ties to Al Qaeda, was released on Tuesday, according to French authorities.

2014-01-01.title: Gaza: Hamas Lawmakers Approve Large Budget Deficit
2014-01-01.abstract: Hamas lawmakers on Tuesday approved a 2014 budget totaling $589 million for the Gaza Strip that included a staggering 75 percent deficit.

2014-01-01.title: Kerry’s Peace Ambitions
2014-01-01.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry’s remarks on Middle East peace talks have changed over time.

2014-01-01.title: Kerry to Press for ‘Framework’ Accord to Keep Mideast Peace Effort Moving
2014-01-01.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry hopes to secure a broad pact encapsulating basic agreements on core issues as a first step, but critics brand the move as an effort to play for time.

2014-01-02.title: Fatal Attacks Strike a Hotel in Somalia
2014-01-02.abstract: No group claimed responsibility for the attacks, despite threatening messages from the Islamist militant group Shabab broadcast on local radio stations in the previous 24 hours.

2014-01-02.title: For Prostitutes Jailed in China, Forced Labor With No Recourse
2014-01-02.abstract: China’s murky penal system for prostitutes is similar to re-education through labor, with women being held in centers for up to two years and often required to work seven days a week for no pay.

2014-01-02.title: Netanyahu Demands Recognition of Israel
2014-01-02.abstract: The Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, has said that Palestinians’ recognition of the Jewish state is an issue that underpins all others.

2014-01-02.title: Sticking Point in Peace Talks: Recognition of a Jewish State
2014-01-02.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu argues that Palestinian recognition of Israel as a Jewish state underpins all other issues in peace negotiations, and this focus is exactly what makes it unacceptable to Palestinians.

2014-01-02.title: Private Schools for Poor Pressured by Right to Education Act
2014-01-02.abstract: Schools that cannot meet the 2009 law’s infrastructure requirements, even if they cannot afford the necessary repairs, are at risk of closure.

2014-01-03.title: As the Working Poor Become More Common in Britain, So Does Hunger
2014-01-03.abstract: Five years of economic weakness, austerity and rising prices have left a mark, with at least 500,000 people in Britain reliant on food aid, three times more than a year ago.

2014-01-03.title: Food Need Grows Among Britain’s Working Poor
2014-01-03.abstract: In Britain, sluggish growth and rising prices have left a mark: earnings have stagnated while the cost of living has gone up by almost 20 percent.

2014-01-03.title: While Kerry Pushes Peace Talks, Israeli Leader Airs Criticism
2014-01-03.abstract: Upon the arrival of Secretary of State John Kerry in Israel, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu questioned the Palestinian commitment to peace.

2014-01-03.title: Central African Republic: Violence Forces Aid Group to Curtail Work
2014-01-03.abstract: Doctors Without Borders said it had drastically scaled back operations at an airport encampment in Bangui, the capital, because of a growing political and sectarian crisis.

2014-01-03.title: Hezbollah Moving Long-Range Missiles From Syria to Lebanon, an Analyst Says
2014-01-03.abstract: The Shiite militia has been moving long-range missiles to Lebanon from bases inside Syria, according to an Israeli national security analyst, who said the missiles could strike deep inside his country.

2014-01-04.title: Mystery in Hezbollah Operative’s Life and Death
2014-01-04.abstract: The assassination of Hassane Laqees, Hezbollah’s master technician and logistics expert, has exposed a convulsing region’s tangled and shifting alliances and enmities.

2014-01-04.title: U.S. Is Facing Hard Choices in South Sudan
2014-01-04.abstract: With no plans for American military intervention, the United States is frantically brokering peace talks between the warring factions while trying to fortify a United Nations peacekeeping force.

2014-01-04.title: A Desperate River Crossing
2014-01-04.abstract: Residents of Bor, a city in the Republic of South Sudan, fled deadly violence there by taking ferries across the White Nile to Awerial, where an estimated 76,000 displaced people are stranded.

2014-01-04.title: Returning of Refugees to Bulgaria Worries U.N.
2014-01-04.abstract: The United Nations refugee agency urged European countries to stop sending back asylum seekers, many of them originally from Syria, because they face poor treatment in Bulgaria.

2014-01-04.title: Deadly Violence Erupts During Rallies Across Egypt
2014-01-04.abstract: At least 13 people were killed as violence broke out during Muslim Brotherhood marches just days before a planned referendum on a draft constitution.

2014-01-05.title: Opposition Party Boycotting Bangladesh Election
2014-01-05.abstract: Amid unrest and despite violence, the governing Awami League is going ahead with Sunday’s vote, while the main opposition party is refusing to take part.

2014-01-05.title: South Sudan and Rebels Open Peace Talks
2014-01-05.abstract: The urgency of the talks was evident as gunfire rattled the nerves of residents in Juba, South Sudan’s capital, on Saturday evening after weeks of relative calm there.

2014-01-05.title: Power Vacuum in Middle East Lifts Militants
2014-01-05.abstract: The bloodshed that has engulfed Iraq, Lebanon and Syria exposes the emergence of a post-American Middle East in which no broker has the will or power to contain the region’s sectarian hatreds.

2014-01-05.title: A Struggle Over the Jordan Valley
2014-01-05.abstract: As a strategic corridor along the West Bank border with Jordan, the Jordan Valley holds deep meaning for many.

2014-01-05.title: Strategic Corridor in West Bank Remains a Stumbling Block in Mideast Talks
2014-01-05.abstract: As negotiators struggle to make headway in peace talks, they remain at odds over the strategic corridor that runs between the populous heartland of the West Bank and the border with Jordan.

2014-01-06.title: Freed Russian Oligarch Leaves Germany for Switzerland With Family
2014-01-06.abstract: A statement from aides to Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky, the former oil tycoon, said he has “not yet made any plans about permanent residency in Switzerland or anywhere else.”

2014-01-06.title: Spain’s Solar Pullback Threatens Pocketbooks
2014-01-06.abstract: The Spanish government has changed its mind on a law offering attractive rates for solar energy, and instead will make producers pay a charge on electricity they generate and use themselves.

2014-01-06.title: Iraqis Make Gains Against Militants in Key City
2014-01-06.abstract: Iraqi security forces and their tribal allies reclaimed parts of Ramadi from Qaeda-linked militants, officials said, but the insurgents appeared to maintain control of much of Falluja.

2014-01-06.title: The Muslim Brotherhood, Back in a Fight to Survive
2014-01-06.abstract: Members of the Brotherhood, now outlawed and underground, said they were trying to bolster their ranks for a long struggle against Egypt’s military-backed government.

2014-01-06.title: Fog-Wreathed Paradise, Built by a Billionaire Under a Cloud
2014-01-06.abstract: Deng Hong, the founder of Jiuzhai Paradise, one of China’s biggest hotels, is a well-connected billionaire now under investigation by the Communist Party’s anti-corruption commission.

2014-01-07.title: After Winning Expo, Emirate Fumes at Allies It Says Didn’t Back It
2014-01-07.abstract: After Pakistan and Afghanistan were accused of casting their votes for other cities in the competition for the 2020 World Expo, the score-settling started.

2014-01-07.title: High Stakes for Sudan in the South’s Conflict
2014-01-07.abstract: With the flow of oil and border security at risk, the longstanding rivals discussed joint patrols of South Sudan’s oil fields, among other measures.

2014-01-07.title: Africans Continue to Protest in Israel
2014-01-07.abstract: Thousands of African asylum seekers flooded the streets of Tel Aviv for a second day on Monday to protest their treatment by the Israeli authorities.

2014-01-07.title: Jordan to Renew Appeal on Syrian Refugees
2014-01-07.abstract: The ambassador to the United Nations from Jordan said he would renew his appeal to the governing body to visit countries that shelter millions of Syrian refugees, including his own.

2014-01-07.title: Bodies of 2 American Climbers Found in Argentina
2014-01-07.abstract: Francis Keenan, 28, and Jarod Von Rueden, 22, were trying to scale Mount Aconcagua when Mr. Von Rueden sent out a distress radio signal Dec. 31.

2014-01-08.title: Iraqis Flee Falluja and Ramadi
2014-01-08.abstract: Terrified civilians escaped fierce fighting between government forces and Sunni militants linked to Al Qaeda.

2014-01-08.title: In Iraqi City Under Siege, More Support for Militants Than Officials
2014-01-08.abstract: As the army prepared an assault on Falluja, many residents said they had more sympathy for the Qaeda-backed militants than for the government.

2014-01-08.title: Ethiopian Migrants Expelled by Saudis Remain in Limbo Back Home
2014-01-08.abstract: Many Ethiopians who were deported after seeking work in Saudi Arabia face bleak prospects at home and put a strain on the government that had benefited from their remittances.

2014-01-08.title: Additional U.S. Battalion Going to South Korea
2014-01-08.abstract: The nine-month deployment of 800 soldiers, battle tanks and other fighting vehicles was not tied to any specific security concern or provocation, the Pentagon said.

2014-01-08.title: U.S. Military Helicopter Crashes on Coast of England
2014-01-08.abstract: All four people aboard were believed to have been killed after a helicopter on a training mission went down in northern Norfolk, the authorities said.

2014-01-09.title: U.S. to List Libyan Groups and Militant Tied to Benghazi Attack as Terrorists
2014-01-09.abstract: The State Department was expected to apply the designation to two groups and an individual, the first formal accusations of responsibility for the 2012 attack that left an American ambassador dead.

2014-01-09.title: Greenpeace in New Feud With Russia Over a Ship Seized by Senegal
2014-01-09.abstract: After freeing a Greenpeace crew, Russia finds one of its ships captured by Senegal in a poaching dispute, but Moscow blames Greenpeace.

2014-01-09.title: With Help of Victims From 1849, Scientists Decode Early Strain of Cholera
2014-01-09.abstract: Scientists in Ontario used bits of human intestine stored in a Philadelphia medical museum to map a cholera strain linked to 19th-century pandemics.

2014-01-09.title: Bird Flu Cited by Officials in Death in Canada
2014-01-09.abstract: In the first known fatality from the illness in North America, a person died in Alberta this month, two days after returning from China, Canada’s health minister said.

2014-01-09.title: Chinese Official Criticizes Japanese Shrine Visit
2014-01-09.abstract: The Chinese ambassador to the United Nations on Wednesday criticized the Japanese prime minister’s recent visit to a controversial shrine as an “affront to international justice.”

2014-01-10.title: New Estimate Sharply Raises Death Toll in South Sudan
2014-01-10.abstract: The International Crisis Group said that the number of dead from the country’s conflict was close to 10,000 people, a major increase from earlier estimates by the United Nations.

2014-01-10.title: A Race for More Than the Mayor of Istanbul
2014-01-10.abstract: As a corruption scandal has imperiled Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s hold on power, the spotlight is shifting to the race as the first step by opponents to challenge him at the ballot box.

2014-01-10.title: French Far-Right Leader Aims for E.U. Parliament
2014-01-10.abstract: Marine Le Pen, the leader of France’s National Front, laid out an ambitious plan to win seats this spring in the European Parliament, with the hope of provoking a “political crisis” there.

2014-01-10.title: Call to Honor Pakistani Boy in Fatal Tackle of a Bomber
2014-01-10.abstract: Aitzaz Hasan, 15, stopped a suicide bomber whose target was believed to be a school in northwestern Pakistan, which has a history of sectarian violence.

2014-01-10.title: U.S. Mission in South Sudan Shows Limits of Military
2014-01-10.abstract: Recent evacuation efforts highlight the remaining gaps in intelligence and communications that American forces face in Africa, commanders and independent analysts said.

2014-01-11.title: Toiling to Bring Rwanda Genocide Suspects to Justice
2014-01-11.abstract: Alain Gauthier and his wife, Dafroza, have spent 13 years collecting evidence against Rwandans living in France whom they suspect of having participated in their country’s 1994 genocide.

2014-01-11.title: Thai Beer Loses Esteem After Heiress’s Remarks
2014-01-11.abstract: Voters in rural Thailand have begun boycotting the popular Singha beer brand because of its affiliation to the leader of antigovernment protests.

2014-01-11.title: U.S. Says Iran Won’t Attend Peace Talks on Syria
2014-01-11.abstract: American officials say Iran has made no effort to use its influence to get the Syrian government to cease bombing and allow humanitarian aid.

2014-01-11.title: World Bank Is Criticized for Honduran Loan
2014-01-11.abstract: The bank’s ombudsman delivered a stinging review of a loan to a palm-oil company engaged in a violent conflict with poor farm workers over land in Honduras.

2014-01-11.title: U.S. Advisers Sent to Help Somalia Fight the Shabab
2014-01-11.abstract: Three military advisers were deployed to Somalia last month to help provide logistics, planning and communication assistance to forces combating the Islamic militant group.

2014-01-12.title: In Scandal, Turkey’s Leaders May Be Losing Their Tight Grip on News Media
2014-01-12.abstract: The Turkish government, which has frequently had journalists fired and even jailed, may be losing its grip on the news media, as a former ally turns and Internet influence abounds.

2014-01-12.abstract: Lago Enriquillo in the Dominican Republic, the largest lake in the Caribbean, has been rising and rising, devouring tens of thousands of acres of farmland, ranches and whatever else stands in its way.

2014-01-12.abstract: After thousands celebrated the resignations of two of the Central African Republic’s leaders, Christian militias renewed their fight with the Muslim rebels known as Seleka.

2014-01-12.abstract: Giorgos Germenis and Panagiotis Iliopoulos, lawmakers from the Golden Dawn party, were ordered to be detained pending trial on charges of joining and directing a criminal organization.

2014-01-12.title: Antigovernment Protesters Try to Shut Down Bangkok
2014-01-12.abstract: The government mobilized the police and soldiers as antigovernment protesters seeking to block next month’s elections took over major roads.

2014-01-13.title: Afghan Police, Often Derided, Face Another Drawback: Missing Pay
2014-01-13.abstract: The case exemplifies a glaring failure in the American-led nation-building effort here: weak government institutions are often staffed by unqualified officials.

2014-01-13.title: Syria Rebels Turn Against Most Radical Group Tied to Al Qaeda
2014-01-13.abstract: A broad array of factions have turned against the Islamic State of Iraq and Syria, known as ISIS, in a showdown over tactics, power and ideology.

2014-01-13.title: Clashes Between Militants and Army Spread in Iraq
2014-01-13.abstract: A confrontation between insurgents and the army proved fatal in Baghdad and Anbar Provinces, and in Baghdad, two car bombs killed more than a dozen civilians.

2014-01-13.title: Saving Relics, Afghans Defy the Taliban
2014-01-13.abstract: At the National Museum of Afghanistan, the most important objects smashed by the Taliban have been reassembled, and looted treasures have also been returned.

2014-01-14.title: China: Village Blast Kills 14

2014-01-14.title: Nigerian President Signs Ban on Same-Sex Relationships
2014-01-14.abstract: The ban signed by President Goodluck Jonathan threatens violators with prison terms, and is considered the most significant setback to gay rights in Africa.

2014-01-14.title: Iraq: Deadly Bombings Erupt in Baghdad
2014-01-14.abstract: At least 29 people were killed as car bombs struck several Shiite neighborhoods in northern Baghdad on Monday.

2014-01-14.abstract: With the United States and Iran set to embark on a critical phase of nuclear talks, President Obama is struggling to prevent Senate Democrats from backing a bill that could thwart his diplomatic efforts.

2014-01-14.title: Japan’s Leader Pledges Aid on Africa Tour
2014-01-14.abstract: Prime Minister Shinzo Abe finished a three-nation visit to Africa during which he offered aid and development projects potentially worth billions of dollars.

2014-01-15.title: N.S.A. Devises Radio Pathway Into Computers
2014-01-15.abstract: The agency has put software in thousands of computers that allows for surveillance and can also create a path for launching cyberattacks, according to documents, experts and officials.

2014-01-15.title: Training Fighters of Future Across Gaza
2014-01-15.abstract: A Hamas program aims to prepare high school students for what some see as the next inevitable clash with Israel.

2014-01-15.title: Mexico Faces Obstacle in Curbing Vigilantes Fighting Drug Gang
2014-01-15.abstract: Spokesmen for the vigilante groups said they would not give up their weapons until the government arrested the top leaders of a drug ring that controls part of Michoacán State.

2014-01-15.abstract: The United Nations human rights chief Navi Pillay condemned the Nigerian law, which gives anyone entering a same-sex union 14 years in jail.

2014-01-15.abstract: Tunisia and Egypt, whose revolts ignited the Arab Spring, provide a dual lesson in the pitfalls and potentials for democracy in the region as they move toward new charters.

2014-01-16.abstract: Qutaiba al-Satem, implicated in a bombing, has become the face of what many Lebanese fear: citizens radicalized by the Syrian conflict. But in the village where he grew up, the picture is murkier.

2014-01-16.abstract: An anti-corruption group was heartened when China’s new president, Xi Jinping, came to power in 2012. Since then, their ideals have collided with a harsh reality.

2014-01-16.title: A Culture of Violence
2014-01-16.abstract: In the South Pacific island nation of Papua New Guinea, one of the world’s most violent places, nearly every woman has experienced some form of domestic abuse.

2014-01-16.title: Obama’s Path From Critic to Overseer of Spying
2014-01-16.abstract: In 2007, Barack Obama described a surveillance state run amok. He is now the leader of a surveillance state that some of his own advisers think has gotten out of control.

2014-01-16.abstract: The American Embassy School, popular with expatriates in New Delhi, is facing questions from the Indian government in the latest volley in its diplomatic spat with the United States.

2014-01-17.abstract: The police in New Delhi arrested a third man on Thursday in connection with the gang-rape of a Danish tourist and said they were still seeking five other men.

2014-01-17.abstract: The United Nations has taken greater steps to protect civilians from the conflict, and while approval for reinforcements has come quickly, the troops and equipment have not.

2014-01-17.title: Climate Aids in Study Face Big Obstacles
2014-01-17.abstract: The latest draft report by United Nations climate experts cites technologies that are still in their infancy, with few projects operating around the world.

2014-01-17.title: Under Gang’s Rule, a Mexican City Loses Hope in the State
2014-01-17.abstract: Apatzingán has been under the control of a gang called the Knights Templar, with little interference from state or federal authority.

2014-01-17.abstract: Congress is placing restrictions on the Obama administration’s plan to shift responsibility for armed drones toward the military and away from the C.I.A.

2014-01-18.title: Video: Papua New Guinea, Where Violence Can Seem Like the Norm for Women
2014-01-18.abstract: The Australian government found that nearly every woman has experienced some form of physical violence, sexual violence or both.

2014-01-18.title: Sanctions Eased, Iran Gets Feelers From Old Trading Partners
2014-01-18.abstract: Excitement over the interim agreement, which will ease some provisions of the American-led sanctions on Iran but essentially leave all of them in place, has not extended to American companies.

2014-01-18.abstract: An unlikely experiment is underway in the Central African Republic as a council of 135 rebels, rivals, politicians and others is trying to choose an interim president.

2014-01-18.title: Bill With Antigay Provisions Is Blocked by Ugandan Leader
2014-01-18.abstract: President Yoweri Museveni was in an uncomfortable position as Western governments that provide aid condemned the bill’s harsh sentences.

2014-01-18.title: 384 Priests Defrocked Over Abuse in 2 Years
2014-01-18.abstract: After the child sexual abuse scandal exploded in Europe and beyond, the Vatican defrocked 260 priests in 2011 and 124 more in 2012, according to statistics compiled by The Associated Press.

2014-01-19.title: Region Boiling, Israel Takes Up Castle Strategy
2014-01-19.abstract: As Israel faces threats both from traditional Shiite adversaries like Iran and from extremist Sunni cells in Syria, Iraq and beyond, officials insist they have no inclination to intervene.

2014-01-19.title: Determined to Miss Nothing, Russia Trains All Eyes on Sochi
2014-01-19.abstract: As the Winter Games approach, tens of thousands of personnel and surveillance cameras have been deployed to Sochi. Critics say the measures threaten to temper the Olympic spirit.

2014-01-19.abstract: Jihad Makdissi, the former Foreign Ministry spokesman, has resurfaced, a rare high-profile dissenter seeking to position himself as a voice for many Syrians who remain on the sidelines.

2014-01-19.title: In Syria, Former Official Says, ‘Nobody Is Winning’
2014-01-19.abstract: Excerpts from an interview with Jihad Makdissi, a former Foreign Ministry spokesman in Syria who fled in 2012 as the government cracked down on protesters.

2014-01-19.title: Deadly Bomb Blast Rocks Pakistan Military Compound
2014-01-19.abstract: Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan, an umbrella group for militants, has taken responsibility for a bomb planted inside a vehicle that killed at least 20 people.

2014-01-20.title: In a City on Okinawa, Mayor’s Re-election Deals a Blow to Marine Base Relocation Plan
2014-01-20.abstract: Voters in an Okinawan city re-elected a mayor who promised to block construction of a replacement site for the United States base.

2014-01-20.abstract: Authorities are struggling to decide how to handle rolezinhos — rowdy gatherings of young people at shopping malls — as questions abound about the racial, social and class implications.

2014-01-20.title: Proposed Abortion Restrictions in Spain Face Backlash
2014-01-20.abstract: The proposal would allow the termination of a pregnancy only if it was the result of rape or if having the baby would endanger the mother’s health.

2014-01-20.title: Egypt Says Twitter Post by Liberal Was Crime
2014-01-20.abstract: One of Egypt’s most prominent liberal intellectuals was charged with insulting the judiciary because he criticized a court ruling against three American nonprofits that promote democracy.

2014-01-20.title: An Attack on Westerners Helps Bridge a Divide in Kabul
2014-01-20.abstract: The attack by the Taliban at a restaurant in Kabul narrowed a gap between the Westerners who live an often cosseted existence, and ordinary Afghans, who have borne the brunt of the war’s violence.

2014-01-21.title: Syria: 14 Companies Bid to Destroy Chemical Weapons
2014-01-21.abstract: The companies, including four from the United States, have bid for contracts to eradicate 500 tons of chemicals in Syria’s munitions stockpile.

2014-01-21.abstract: Highly radioactive water found in a reactor building at the Fukushima nuclear plant indicates that the reactor’s containment vessel was breached during the accident, the plant’s operator said Monday.

2014-01-21.title: Greece: Prison Escapee Vows to Fight Again
2014-01-21.abstract: Christodoulos Xiros, a leftist guerrilla who walked away from prison while on furlough this month, on Monday announced his return to terrorism.

2014-01-21.abstract: Two Navy warships will be in the Black Sea during the Olympics in Sochi, Russia, next month, should they be needed to evacuate Americans, a Pentagon official said.

2014-01-21.abstract: François Hollande once vowed to be on his most exemplary behavior as president of France. But an affair has exposed a more politically calculating side and threatens to undercut his economic agenda.

2014-01-22.title: Visiting Scene, Court Calls Forth Memories of Massacre at a Mall
2014-01-22.abstract: A chief magistrate led a grim field trip to the Westgate mall in Nairobi, Kenya, the scene of a massacre, bringing along prosecutors, defense lawyers and even the suspects.

2014-01-22.abstract: Anzio is engraved in history books as the main landing site of forces that went on to liberate Rome. Now nearby Nettuno is seeking to stake its claim.

2014-01-22.abstract: He called the accusation by congressional leaders “false,” saying he had “clearly and unambiguously acted alone, with no assistance from anyone, much less a government.”

2014-01-22.abstract: A precise diplomatic sequence that relied Iran and the United States to perform failed, forcing Ban Ki-moon, the United Nations secretary general, to stumble.

2014-01-22.title: More Problems for American Couple Accused in Qatar
2014-01-22.abstract: An American accused with his wife of starving their daughter to death says his employer asked him to return to work, even though the couple could face the death penalty.

2014-01-23.title: At Resting Place for France’s ‘Great Men,’ Calls to Include More Women
2014-01-23.abstract: Officials have been barraged with suggestions to add a woman to the greats buried in the Panthéon, where Marie Curie is the only woman interred on her own merit.

2014-01-23.title: State Dept. Learned in November of Photos Said to Show Torture in Syria
2014-01-23.abstract: The photos seem unlikely to change the American policy of seeking a settlement to remove President Bashar al-Assad but avoiding direct military action.

2014-01-23.abstract: Advocates argue that a diplomatic window created by the nuclear deal is a new opportunity to push for the release of the former Marine, Amir Hekmati.

2014-01-23.abstract: Nearly 500 police officers were either fired or reassigned Wednesday in what appears to be a purge related to a graft inquiry that has already ensnared many of the prime minister’s allies.

2014-01-23.abstract: Iran reportedly hanged 40 people in the first two weeks of January, 33 of them in the last week, leading United Nations human rights experts to call for a halt to the executions.

2014-01-24.title: Number of Darfur’s Displaced Surged in 2013
2014-01-24.abstract: A report by the top United Nations peacekeeping official to the Security Council presented a pessimistic picture of the decade-old Darfur conflict.

2014-01-24.abstract: A report by the Defense Science Board suggests using some of the techniques that the National Security Agency has employed against terrorists.

2014-01-24.title: On Moroccan Hill, Villagers Make Stand Against a Mine
2014-01-24.abstract: An unlikely spot for a settlement was established to protest a mining company’s expropriation of precious water supplies, as well as the pollution that results from the mining.

2014-01-24.title: Cuban Vendors, in Rare Move, Stage a Protest
2014-01-24.abstract: In a rare demonstration of dissent, dozens of Cuban artisans and vendors protested government inspectors in the city of Holguín, witnesses said.

2014-01-24.title: U.S. Warns Against Business With Iran
2014-01-24.abstract: The warning came as the Treasury announced a settlement with a European bank for having allowed Iran to bypass sanctions by using the company’s access to American banks.

2014-01-25.title: Writer Tells Africa What He Couldn’t Tell ‘Mum’
2014-01-25.abstract: The Kenyan author Binyavanga Wainaina’s decision to come out as gay had added to the fierce debate over homosexuality in sub-Saharan Africa.

2014-01-25.title: South Sudanese Rebels Accuse Government of Ignoring Day-Old Cease-Fire
2014-01-25.abstract: A government spokesman denies the claims that opposition positions were attacked, while analysts say that enforcing the cease-fire will prove more difficult than signing it.

2014-01-25.title: Iran’s Message at Davos Has Eerie Echo
2014-01-25.abstract: President Hassan Rouhani’s speech this week, professing peaceful intentions and a desire for dialogue, invited comparisons to Mohammad Khatami’s attempt at change 10 years ago.

2014-01-25.title: Kerry Presses Iranians to Prove Nuclear Work Is for Peaceful Purposes
2014-01-25.abstract: John Kerry told the World Economic Forum that Iran must accept extensive verification of its nuclear program, abandon plans for a plutonium-producing reactor, and resolve inspectors’ concerns about past compliance.

2014-01-25.title: Star Envoy’s Frankness Puts Kennedy Mystique to Test in Japan
2014-01-25.abstract: Japan swooned when Caroline Kennedy arrived as the United States’ ambassador, but she has surprised her hosts with her frankness on delicate issues, including a controversial dolphin hunt.

2014-01-26.title: Philippines and Rebels Agree on Peace Accord to End Insurgency
2014-01-26.abstract: The agreement will create an autonomous Muslim-dominated region in the restive south of the predominantly Christian country.

2014-01-26.title: Holocaust Told in One Word, 6 Million Times
2014-01-26.abstract: The book “And Every Single One Was Someone” — meant as a kind of coffee-table monument or conversation starter — consists of the single word “Jew,” printed six million times.

2014-01-26.title: Nepal Lawmakers Move Closer to Choosing a Prime Minister
2014-01-26.abstract: The country’s Supreme Court dismissed calls for an immediate election and allowed President Ram Baran Yadav to remain in office for now.

2014-01-26.title: India Dismisses Charges Against Soldiers in Killings
2014-01-26.abstract: The army dismissed charges against five soldiers in a case from 2000 in which soldiers were accused of rounding up five Kashmiri men, then shooting and burning them.

2014-01-26.title: China Sentences Legal Activist to 4 Years for Role in Protests
2014-01-26.abstract: Xu Zhiyong campaigned against official corruption and social injustice as part of the New Citizens Movement.

2014-01-27.title: U.N. Inquiry Criticizes Qatar’s Justice System
2014-01-27.abstract: A critique concludes that prosecutors and judges need more autonomy, defense lawyers should have more access to evidence, and foreign suspects may be vulnerable to discrimination.

2014-01-27.title: Militants Down Egyptian Helicopter, Killing 5 Soldiers
2014-01-27.abstract: Islamist militants shot down an Egyptian military helicopter in the Sinai Peninsula with a surface-to-air missile over the weekend, raising new alarms about the terrorist insurgency there.

2014-01-27.title: Ukraine Protests Spread as Overture Is Spurned
2014-01-27.abstract: The growing unrest raised the prospect of widening violence and deepening political chaos while leaders in Kiev, the capital, remain at an impasse.

2014-01-27.title: Peace Deal to End Insurgency Came After Philippine Leader’s Ultimatum to Rebels
2014-01-27.abstract: President Benigno S. Aquino III made it clear to Muslim rebels that the peace deal he was offering was the best they could expect from any Philippine leader.

2014-01-27.title: Three Years After Uprising, Tunisia Approves Constitution
2014-01-27.abstract: The country’s Assembly voted its approval on Sunday, completing a two-year drafting process and ushering in a new democratic era.

2014-01-28.title: Lebanese Cringe as a Breakdown in Government Wafts Uphill
2014-01-28.abstract: Since the government resigned 10 months ago, Parliament has scarcely convened, no major laws have been passed and trash overflows in Beirut — forcing residents to live with the stench.

2014-01-28.title: Former Exit Port for a Wave of Cubans Hopes to Attract Global Shipping
2014-01-28.abstract: The Mariel port, where desperate refugees once crammed onto boats to flee the country, has been upgraded at a cost of about $900 million and holds the government’s hopes of connecting Cuba and the rest of the world.

2014-01-28.title: Syria Talks Fail to Yield Pact to Lift Aid Blockade
2014-01-28.abstract: Little progress was seen in the third day of talks on authorizing an international convoy to enter blockaded areas in the city of Homs.

2014-01-28.title: At Neutral Site, Syrians Feel Free to Confront the Other Side
2014-01-28.abstract: Frank, even tense, encounters in Geneva have in some ways been the most significant action at peace talks that have been more about optics than results.

2014-01-28.title: Second Cargo of Chemical Agents Leaves Syria as Pace Is Criticized
2014-01-28.abstract: The concern is that the schedule of deadlines to destroy the weapons stockpile is falling dangerously behind.

2014-01-29.title: After Crisis, Greeks Work to Promote ‘Social’ Economy
2014-01-29.abstract: Businesses tired of dealing with bureaucrats and failing corporations are relying on volunteer networks to provide cheaper goods and to sell directly to customers.

2014-01-29.title: Afghan Justice Proves Complicated in Killing of 9-Year-Old Boy
2014-01-29.abstract: The rule of law is a fragile concept in Afghanistan as shown in a case involving a prominent businessman, his friend on the court, and support from both the government and the Taliban.

2014-01-29.title: Rebels in Syria Claim Control of Resources
2014-01-29.abstract: Islamist rebels and extremists are using oil and gas proceeds to underwrite their fights against one another as well as President Bashar al-Assad, American officials said.

2014-01-29.title: Iran: U.N. to Visit Uranium Mine
2014-01-29.abstract: The visit by United Nations inspectors is part of a temporary international agreement aimed at resolving the longstanding dispute over the Iranian nuclear program.

2014-01-29.title: China: Chicken Sales Suspended Over Concerns About Avian Flu
2014-01-29.abstract: As the Chinese get ready for Lunar New Year feasts, several areas in China have banned the sale of live chickens, as fears grew over the spread of avian influenza.

2014-01-30.title: Despite Decades of Enmity, Israel Quietly Aids Syrian Civilians
2014-01-30.abstract: The humanitarian disaster in Syria is eroding once-impervious psychological barriers between citizens of two bordering nations still technically at war.

2014-01-30.title: Syrian Opposition’s Calm at Talks Surprises as Officials Falter
2014-01-30.abstract: A turnabout has the exile coalition and its Western backers declaring an early, if modest, victory.

2014-01-30.title: U.S. Says Russia Tested Missile, Despite Treaty
2014-01-30.abstract: American officials believe Russia tested a new ground-launched cruise missile, raising concerns about Moscow’s compliance with an arms deal that has been viewed as a bedrock accord in ending the Cold War.

2014-01-30.title: New Government for Czech Republic
2014-01-30.abstract: President Milos Zeman of the Czech Republic appointed a new center-left government on Wednesday, ending a protracted period of political paralysis that has destabilized the country.

2014-01-30.title: Iraq: More Violence in Baghdad
2014-01-30.abstract: Car bombings and shootings in Baghdad on Wednesday killed at least 19 people, according to security and medical officials, adding to a death toll that has been rising quickly.

2014-01-31.title: Syria Talks May Yield Little, but Give Cover to Those Defying Hard-Liners
2014-01-31.abstract: Government supporters complained that the opposition delegation could not claim to represent all opponents, let alone all Syrians, while opposition activists said they would look weak if they failed to win concessions.

2014-01-31.title: Its Great Lake Shriveled, Iran Confronts Crisis of Water Supply
2014-01-31.abstract: President Hassan Rouhani has identified water as a national security issue, and nowhere is the crisis more pronounced than at Lake Urmia, once one of the largest salt lakes in the world.

2014-01-31.title: Hong Kong Paper Ousts Top Editor, Stirring Concern
2014-01-31.abstract: Ming Pao’s history as a serious, independent paper has given it outsize influence, but it might be the latest victim of the mainland’s effort to rein in the media here.

2014-01-31.title: Panama: 32 of 35 Detained North Koreans to Be Freed
2014-01-31.abstract: The crew members of a North Korean freighter have been held since July, when Soviet-era jets, engines and antiaircraft missiles from Cuba were found on board.

2014-01-31.title: Egypt Tries to Reassure Journalists From Abroad
2014-01-31.abstract: The government told foreign correspondents that they are free to work in Egypt after prosecutors accused 20 journalists for Al Jazeera television of conspiring with the Muslim Brotherhood.
